evals.prompt: Add MatrixScan Count to my Capacitor app. Here's my entry point: EmptyApp.js. I need to scan EAN-13 and Code 128 barcodes and show the scan result count after each shutter press.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads integration.md, writes a complete BarcodeCount integration into EmptyApp.js including plugin init, context creation, camera setup using BarcodeCount.recommendedCameraSettings, BarcodeCountSettings with EAN-13 and Code 128 enabled, new BarcodeCount(settings) + context.addMode, a didScan listener that logs or displays the recognized barcode count, BarcodeCountView connected to the DOM element, camera on, and barcodeCount.isEnabled = true. Shows the setup checklist.

assertions.text: Setup checklist is shown (mentions scandit-capacitor-datacapture-core, scandit-capacitor-datacapture-barcode, and npx cap sync)
Setup checklist mentions iOS Info.plist NSCameraUsageDescription
await ScanditCaptureCorePlugin.initializePlugins() is present and awaited before any other Scandit call
DataCaptureContext.initialize( is present
'-- ENTER YOUR SCANDIT LICENSE KEY HERE --' is present
BarcodeCount.recommendedCameraSettings is used (static getter or createRecommendedCameraSettings)
Camera.withSettings( is called
context.setFrameSource(camera) is called
new BarcodeCountSettings() is present
Symbology.EAN13UPCA is enabled
Symbology.Code128 is enabled
Only Symbology.EAN13UPCA and Symbology.Code128 are enabled in the BarcodeCountSettings (semantic)
new BarcodeCount(settings) is present (context NOT passed to BarcodeCount constructor)
context.addMode( is called with barcodeCount
barcodeCount.addListener( is called with a didScan callback
session.recognizedBarcodes is accessed inside the didScan callback
new BarcodeCountView( or BarcodeCountView.forContextWithMode( is present
connectToElement( is called on the barcodeCountView
barcodeCount.isEnabled = true is present
camera.switchToDesiredState(FrameSourceState.On) is called
Import from 'scandit-capacitor-datacapture-core' is present
Import from 'scandit-capacitor-datacapture-barcode' is present

evals.prompt: Count items off a packing slip using MatrixScan Count. Here's ProductListApp.js. When the user presses the shutter, add all recognized barcodes to the packingList array and re-render the list. Highlight items not on the expected list with a different color (red dot vs green dot).
evals.expected_output: The skill adds BarcodeCount to ProductListApp.js. The didScan listener pushes recognized barcodes into packingList and calls renderPackingList. A BarcodeCountCaptureList is set up with sample target barcodes. The view uses BarcodeCountViewStyle.Dot, with recognizedBrush set to green and notInListBrush set to red. Existing DOM state and render functions are preserved.

assertions.text: await ScanditCaptureCorePlugin.initializePlugins() is present
DataCaptureContext.initialize( is present
BarcodeCount.recommendedCameraSettings is used
new BarcodeCount(settings) is present
context.addMode( is called with barcodeCount
barcodeCount.addListener( is called with a didScan callback
session.recognizedBarcodes is accessed in the didScan callback
packingList or equivalent array is populated with recognized barcodes from the session
renderPackingList() is called after adding barcodes
BarcodeCountCaptureList.create( is used to define target barcodes
TargetBarcode.create( is used to define at least one target barcode
barcodeCount.setBarcodeCountCaptureList( is called
BarcodeCountViewStyle.Dot is used (required for brush customization)
recognizedBrush is set to a green-ish Brush
notInListBrush is set to a red-ish Brush
connectToElement( is called on barcodeCountView
barcodeCount.isEnabled = true is set
Existing packingList variable is still referenced and used in the integrated code (semantic)
Existing renderPackingList function is preserved
Existing updateTotal / totalLabel logic is preserved
Existing clearButton / onClearTapped logic is preserved

evals.prompt: I want to use BarcodeCountView in a fixed-position div via connectToElement in my Capacitor app. The div has id 'scan-area'. After scanning is done, I need to detach the view.
evals.expected_output: The skill integrates BarcodeCount with BarcodeCountView.connectToElement pointing to document.getElementById('scan-area'), and teardown calls barcodeCountView.detachFromElement().

assertions.text: connectToElement(document.getElementById('scan-area')) or equivalent is called on barcodeCountView
detachFromElement() is called on barcodeCountView in the teardown/cleanup function
new BarcodeCountView( or BarcodeCountView.forContextWithMode( is present
barcodeCount.isEnabled = true is set
camera.switchToDesiredState(FrameSourceState.On) is present
camera.switchToDesiredState(FrameSourceState.Off) is present in teardown
The view is connected to the 'scan-area' element (not 'data-capture-view') (semantic)
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCount, camera) is still referenced and used (semantic)

evals.prompt: I want to hide the toolbar and exit button to keep the MatrixScan Count UI minimal. Just show the shutter button and hints.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets shouldShowToolbar = false and shouldShowExitButton = false on the BarcodeCountView. Other UI elements remain at their defaults.

assertions.text: barcodeCountView.shouldShowToolbar = false is set
barcodeCountView.shouldShowExitButton = false is set
shouldShowShutterButton is NOT set to false (shutter button should remain visible)
shouldShowHints is NOT set to false (hints should remain visible)
The explanation mentions these are BarcodeCountView properties (semantic)
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCount, barcodeCountView) is still referenced and used (semantic)

evals.prompt: Localize all on-screen hints in my MatrixScan Count app to French.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets all textFor*Hint properties on BarcodeCountView to French strings, including textForTapShutterToScanHint, textForScanningHint, textForMoveCloserAndRescanHint, textForMoveFurtherAndRescanHint, and textForTapToUncountHint. Also sets exitButtonText and clearHighlightsButtonText.

assertions.text: view.textForTapShutterToScanHint is set to a French string
view.textForScanningHint is set to a French string
view.textForMoveCloserAndRescanHint is set to a French string
view.textForMoveFurtherAndRescanHint is set to a French string
At least 4 distinct textFor*Hint properties are set
All assigned hint text strings appear to be in French (semantic)
exitButtonText or clearHighlightsButtonText is also set to French (semantic)
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCount, barcodeCountView) is still referenced and used (semantic)

evals.prompt: Show a stock-status overlay on each scanned barcode using BarcodeCountStatusProvider. Barcodes starting with 'EXP' should show Expired status, 'LOW' should show LowStock, others should show None. Status icons should appear immediately after scanning without requiring the user to tap a button.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets shouldShowStatusIconsOnScan = true on the view, calls view.setStatusProvider with an object implementing onStatusRequested, maps barcode data to BarcodeCountStatus values (Expired, LowStock, None), builds BarcodeCountStatusItem.create for each barcode, and calls callback.onStatusReady(BarcodeCountStatusResultSuccess.create(...)).

assertions.text: view.shouldShowStatusIconsOnScan = true is set
view.setStatusProvider( is called with an object
onStatusRequested is a method on the provider object
BarcodeCountStatusItem.create( is called for each barcode
BarcodeCountStatus.Expired is used for barcodes starting with 'EXP'
BarcodeCountStatus.LowStock is used for barcodes starting with 'LOW'
BarcodeCountStatus.None is used as the default status
BarcodeCountStatusResultSuccess.create( is called with the status items
callback.onStatusReady( is called with the result
A note or comment mentions this feature is beta (Cap ≥8.3) (semantic)
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCount, barcodeCountView) is still referenced and used (semantic)

evals.prompt: Allow tap-to-uncount in my MatrixScan Count app so users can deselect items by tapping their highlight. Show a French hint when they do it.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets tapToUncountEnabled = true on the BarcodeCountView and sets textForTapToUncountHint to a French string.

assertions.text: view.tapToUncountEnabled = true is set
view.textForTapToUncountHint is set to a French string
The French hint is a non-empty localized string (semantic)
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCount, barcodeCountView) is still referenced and used (semantic)

evals.prompt: Add a hardware trigger control so users can scan by pressing the volume button (iOS) or XCover hardware button (Android) in my MatrixScan Count app.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets hardwareTriggerEnabled = true for iOS and calls enableHardwareTrigger(null) guarded by BarcodeCountView.hardwareTriggerSupported for Android, with a note that these are platform-specific.

assertions.text: view.hardwareTriggerEnabled = true is set (iOS volume-button trigger)
view.enableHardwareTrigger( is called (Android hardware trigger)
BarcodeCountView.hardwareTriggerSupported is checked before calling enableHardwareTrigger (Android guard)
A comment or note explains that hardwareTriggerEnabled is iOS-only and enableHardwareTrigger is Android-only (semantic)
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCount, barcodeCountView) is still referenced and used (semantic)
